behavior_summary: FIRESHRO::look: When a player/actor 'looks at' (examines) the fire shroom item it sets item info/exclude state (engine housekeeping) and causes the item to 'bark' the label "fire shroom ", i.e., present its name/description to the viewer.

FIRESHRO::gotHit and FIRESHRO::hit: On damage/hit events these handlers guard on an item-local quantity/flag (Item::getQ). If allowed they set that flag to 1 (Item::setQ(1)), inspect the source/attacker to ensure it is not an excluded actor (via Item::isNpc and Npc::isDead checks), and — if checks permit — spawn METHOD::0676 with a byte flag=1 and the item pointer, which appears to activate the fire-shroom effect (explosion/damage/activation).
notable_control_flow: Look path: immediate info/exclude housekeeping then a single external call Item::bark("fire shroom ") and return.
Hit/gotHit path: early gate read from Item::getQ; if the gate fails the handler returns early. When progressing, Item::setQ(1) is called to record/lock the state so subsequent hits act differently.

After setting the Q flag, the handlers validate the incoming source/attacker argument by calling Item::isNpc(source) and, if true, Npc::isDead(source); the alive/dead and NPC checks form a conditional that can prevent spawning.
If the conditional passes, the script spawns METHOD::0676 with parameters (byte=1, this_item) to trigger the shroom's active effect. A small temp local is used to pop/discard the spawn return value.
open_questions: What is the exact gameplay effect of METHOD::0676 (spawned by hit/gotHit)? It is inferred to be the fire-shroom activation (explosion, AoE damage, ignite) but the method body is not present.
What precise semantics/value domain does Item::getQ represent for this item (a cooldown flag, armed state, or stack count)? The handlers treat it like a boolean/guard.
Are there any additional callers or external systems that clear the Item::getQ flag so the shroom can be re-activated? That lifecycle is not visible here.
Which game events map to 'hit' vs 'gotHit' for this item (player attack vs environmental damage) and are there timing differences between them?

main_function.name: U8P_FIRESHRO::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the item’s examination behavior: perform standard item info/exclude housekeeping and display/speak the item's label "fire shroom " to the inspecting process/player.
entry_conditions: Event 0 — triggered when an actor inspects/looks at the fire shroom item (examine/U8P_FIRESHRO::look event)

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESHRO::look
function_map.role: Examine/inspect handler for the fire shroom item; produces a visible/audible label and performs item housekeeping.

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESHRO::gotHit
function_map.role: Handler for gotHit/damage events; gates on item Q flag, sets Q to 1, validates attacker (NPC/alive checks), and conditionally spawns METHOD::0676 to activate the shroom.

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESHRO::hit
function_map.role: Handler for hit events; effectively duplicates gotHit behavior (same gating, attacker checks, and spawn).

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: External API to display/speak a short text string from an item/process (used by U8P_FIRESHRO::look).

function_map.name: Item::getQ
function_map.role: External API returning an item-local quantity/flag used as a gate by hit/gotHit.

function_map.name: Item::setQ
function_map.role: External API to set the item-local quantity/flag (used to set the value to 1 after processing a hit).

function_map.name: Item::isNpc
function_map.role: External predicate to test whether a pointer/argument refers to an NPC (used to decide whether to call Npc::isDead).

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: External predicate to test whether an NPC is dead; used to exclude dead NPC attackers from spawning the effect.

function_map.name: spawn METHOD::0676
function_map.role: Spawn/activate an external method (inferred to be the fire-shroom's active effect) with a flag and the item pointer.

external_calls.purpose: Present the item's descriptive label/string "fire shroom " to the inspecting process/player.
Read a guard/quantity flag to decide whether to process the hit and potentially activate the shroom.
Set the item's Q flag to 1 to record that the hit-processing/activation path has been taken.
Determine whether the source/attacker is an NPC so the script can apply additional dead/alive checks.
If the source is an NPC, check whether it is dead; used to prevent spawning for dead NPC attackers.
Trigger the fire-shroom's active effect (inferred) with parameters (byte=1, this_item) when gating/attacker checks pass.
Same gating read as gotHit; decide whether to proceed with activation.
Same effect as gotHit: set the Q flag to 1 after processing.
Same spawn activation as gotHit when checks pass.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: FIRESHRO::look: When a player/actor 'looks at' (examines) the fire shroom item it sets item info/exclude state (engine housekeeping) and causes the item to 'bark' the label "fire shroom ", i.e., present its name/description to the viewer.
